source: Learning Rsync - inotify-tools installation and use: inotifywait options, event types, and the real-time sync script

questions.why: -m = continuous monitoring; -r recursive, -q quiet, -e event types.

questions.q: Which event type fires when a file is created in the monitored directory?
questions.why: create fires on a new file/dir; modify on content writes; access on reads.

questions.q: How is inotifywait combined with rsync for real-time synchronization?
options: Pipe inotifywait -mrq -e ... into a `while read` loop that runs rsync on each event
Use a for loop over files
questions.why: The event stream drives an rsync run per change: inotifywait ... | while read ...; do rsync ...; done.

questions.q: You can add which rsync option in that script to also remove deleted files at the target?
questions.why: --delete makes the target mirror the source, removing files deleted at the source.

questions.q: How do you make the sync script start at boot?
questions.why: The book adds the script to /etc/rc.local and chmods it executable.
